subject: Wrapper proof complete: add /wrapper/stats for truthful pod VRAM/RAM
body:
Proof from a live L4 pod is in. Please treat this as the wrapper/build contract for the status-bar remote memory monitor.

What is proven:
- RunPod app route GET /runpod/pods/:id returns pod capacity and metadata only. In the tested live payload there was no usable live RAM/VRAM telemetry, and GET /remote/pod/stats currently falls back to {success:false, reason:"telemetry_unavailable"}.
- Inside the pod, VRAM is available truthfully via nvidia-smi.
- Inside the pod, container-scoped RAM budget/usage is available truthfully via cgroup v2, not via free -h.

Live proof values from the pod:
- nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total,memory.used,utilization.gpu --format=csv,noheader,nounits worked and tracked generation load.
- /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current = current container memory usage bytes.
- /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max = container memory limit bytes.
- /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.stat contains inactive_file, so we can optionally compute a working-set-style RAM used value.

Requested wrapper change:
- File: c:\AI\Mpi\mpi-ci\cubric-vision-pod\wrapper\wrapper.py
- Add token-gated GET /wrapper/stats
- Response contract:
  {
    "success": true,
    "source": "wrapper",
    "ram": {
      "total": <bytes>,
      "used": <bytes>,
      "percent": <number>
    },
    "vram": {
      "name": "NVIDIA L4",
      "total": <bytes>,
      "used": <bytes>,
      "percent": <number>,
      "gpuUtilPercent": <number>,
      "available": true
    }
  }

Implementation details:
- VRAM: parse nvidia-smi query-gpu=name,memory.total,memory.used,utilization.gpu and convert MiB to bytes.
- RAM total: read /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max; if value is "max", report null/omit percent rather than lying.
- RAM used: prefer one of these, but please record which you ship:
  1. strict container usage = memory.current
  2. working-set style = memory.current - inactive_file (inactive_file from memory.stat)
- RAM percent = used / total when total is finite.
- On failure, return a truthful unavailable envelope rather than fake 0s.

Why this matters:
- App-side work for MPI-98 is already wired to poll /remote/pod/stats in remote mode and show Pod N/A when live telemetry is unavailable.
- The current RunPod REST guess path is not a viable source of live usage, so this wrapper endpoint is the minimal correct rebuild seam.

One extra issue found while proving this:
- The local route GET /runpod/pods/:id exposed env secrets in the raw pod JSON. I patched Cubric-Vision to redact env values on that route. The user should rotate the leaked RunPod API key / pod token, but there is no wrapper action needed for that beyond the usual fresh pod/token lifecycle.

If you want parity with the RunPod console memory number, my best guess is to use memory.current - inactive_file. If you want the strict container budget view, use memory.current directly. Either is acceptable for MPI-98 as long as the endpoint is explicit and truthful.

recent_events.note: Received; now own MPI-98. /wrapper/stats is a NEW wrapper change = needs a future image rebuild (NOT in v0.4.2 just shipped). Will fold into the next wrapper rebuild batch. Noted the secret-redaction patch + API-key/token rotation flag for the user.
Flagged a 1-line file collision: my uncommitted `path: out.path` hunk lives in their in-progress remoteProxy.js — asked them to keep it (interpolate fix depends on it).

recent_events.outcome: IMPLEMENTED. GET /wrapper/stats added to wrapper.py per the contract (cgroup-v2 working-set RAM + nvidia-smi VRAM, token-gated, truthful 503 envelope; ships working-set = memory.current - inactive_file, matching the RunPod console). App /remote/pod/stats now tries the wrapper FIRST, REST as old-image fallback. Versions bumped image v0.4.3 / wrapper 0.2.6. MPI-92 (Codex, done) confirmed = the secret-audit card; full MPI-98 now owned here. Awaits the v0.4.3 build (user-gated) to go live. Code unverified on a live Pod until then.
